owasp_webapp_page_title: OWASP Web Application Top 10 (2025) - API Security Reference Hub
owasp_webapp_subtitle: The 10 most critical web application security risks and how to mitigate them.
owasp_webapp_overview_title: What is the OWASP Web Application Top 10?
owasp_webapp_overview_desc: The OWASP Top 10 is the most widely recognized awareness document for web application security. The 2025 edition reflects the latest threat landscape, introducing new categories such as Software Supply Chain Failures and Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions.
owasp_webapp_a01_overview_desc: Access control enforces policy such that users cannot act outside of their intended permissions. Failures typically lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction of data, or performing a business function outside the user's limits.
owasp_webapp_a01_risk_desc: Attackers can exploit access control flaws to access other users' accounts, view sensitive files, modify other users' data, or change access rights.
owasp_webapp_a01_check1: Deny access by default except for public resources
owasp_webapp_a01_check2: Implement server-side access control checks on every request
owasp_webapp_a01_check3: Enforce record ownership rather than accepting user-submitted IDs
owasp_webapp_a01_check4: Log access control failures and alert administrators on repeated violations
owasp_webapp_a02_overview_desc: Security misconfiguration occurs when security settings are defined, implemented, or maintained incorrectly. This includes missing security hardening, unnecessary features enabled, default accounts with unchanged passwords, overly verbose error messages, and misconfigured HTTP security headers.
owasp_webapp_a02_risk_desc: Misconfigured servers, frameworks, or cloud services can expose sensitive data, enable unauthorized access, or provide attackers with information to plan further attacks.
owasp_webapp_a02_check1: Implement a repeatable hardening process for all environments
owasp_webapp_a02_check2: Remove or disable unnecessary features, frameworks, and default accounts
owasp_webapp_a02_check3: Configure proper security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options)
owasp_webapp_a02_check4: Use automated tools to verify the effectiveness of configurations
owasp_webapp_a03_heading: 3️⃣ A03 - Software Supply Chain Failures
owasp_webapp_a03_overview_desc: New in 2025. Focuses on risks related to third-party components, dependencies, and CI/CD pipelines. Attackers target the software supply chain by compromising packages, injecting malicious code into open-source libraries, or exploiting build pipeline vulnerabilities.
owasp_webapp_a03_risk_desc: A single compromised dependency can affect thousands of applications. Supply chain attacks can lead to data theft, backdoor installation, or complete system compromise with minimal detection.
owasp_webapp_a03_check1: Pin dependency versions and use lock files for reproducible builds
owasp_webapp_a03_check2: Enable automated dependency scanning (Dependabot, Snyk, Renovate)
owasp_webapp_a03_check3: Generate and maintain Software Bill of Materials (SBOM)
owasp_webapp_a03_check4: Verify package provenance and signatures before inclusion
owasp_webapp_a04_overview_desc: Failures related to cryptography that often lead to exposure of sensitive data. This includes using deprecated algorithms, weak key generation, missing encryption for data in transit or at rest, and improper certificate validation.
owasp_webapp_a04_risk_desc: Weak or missing encryption can expose passwords, credit card numbers, health records, personal information, and business secrets, potentially leading to regulatory violations (GDPR, PCI DSS).
owasp_webapp_a04_check1: Use strong, current algorithms (AES-256, bcrypt/argon2, SHA-256+)
owasp_webapp_a04_check2: Encrypt all sensitive data in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest
owasp_webapp_a04_check3: Never use deprecated algorithms (MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4)
owasp_webapp_a04_check4: Implement proper key management with rotation policies
owasp_webapp_a05_overview_desc: Injection flaws occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query. SQL injection, NoSQL injection, OS command injection, and Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) are the most common forms. Hostile data can trick the interpreter into executing unintended commands.
owasp_webapp_a05_risk_desc: Injection can result in data loss, corruption, unauthorized access, complete host takeover, or denial of service. SQL injection alone remains one of the most dangerous and prevalent attack vectors.
owasp_webapp_a05_check1: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements for all database operations
owasp_webapp_a05_check2: Use context-aware output encoding to prevent XSS
owasp_webapp_a05_check3: Validate and sanitize all user inputs on the server side
owasp_webapp_a05_check4: Use ORMs with parameterized queries rather than raw SQL string building
owasp_webapp_a06_overview_desc: Insecure design refers to risks related to design and architectural flaws. It calls for the use of threat modeling, secure design patterns, and reference architectures. An insecure design cannot be fixed by a perfect implementation; the security controls needed were never created to defend against specific attacks.
owasp_webapp_a06_risk_desc: Design flaws can lead to business logic vulnerabilities that are difficult to detect with automated tools. Missing rate limits on sensitive operations, lack of multi-factor authentication for critical actions, or insufficient fraud detection are all design-level failures.
owasp_webapp_a06_check1: Integrate threat modeling into the development lifecycle
owasp_webapp_a06_check2: Apply rate limiting and anti-automation controls on sensitive operations
owasp_webapp_a06_check3: Use secure design patterns and reference architectures
owasp_webapp_a06_check4: Write unit and integration tests for abuse cases, not just use cases
owasp_webapp_a07_overview_desc: Confirmation of a user's identity, authentication, and session management is critical. Authentication failures occur when applications allow credential stuffing, brute force, weak passwords, or have flawed session management such as non-rotating session IDs after login.
owasp_webapp_a07_risk_desc: Attackers can gain access to user accounts through automated credential stuffing, brute force, or session hijacking. Compromised accounts can lead to identity theft, fraud, and data breaches.
owasp_webapp_a07_check1: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all critical operations
owasp_webapp_a07_check2: Enforce brute force protection with rate limiting and account lockout
owasp_webapp_a07_check3: Rotate session IDs after authentication and use secure session flags
owasp_webapp_a07_check4: Use generic error messages to prevent user enumeration
owasp_webapp_a08_heading: 8️⃣ A08 - Software or Data Integrity Failures
owasp_webapp_a08_overview_desc: Software and data integrity failures relate to code and infrastructure that do not protect against integrity violations. This includes insecure deserialization, using untrusted CDNs or plugins without integrity verification, and auto-update mechanisms without signed updates.
owasp_webapp_a08_risk_desc: Insecure deserialization can lead to remote code execution. Loading scripts from untrusted CDNs without Subresource Integrity (SRI) can allow attackers to inject malicious code into your application.
owasp_webapp_a08_check1: Use Subresource Integrity (SRI) for all external scripts and stylesheets
owasp_webapp_a08_check2: Never use unsafe deserialization (pickle, yaml.load) on untrusted input
owasp_webapp_a08_check3: Verify digital signatures on software updates and CI/CD artifacts
owasp_webapp_a08_check4: Implement integrity checks in CI/CD pipelines to prevent tampering
owasp_webapp_a09_heading: 9️⃣ A09 - Security Logging & Alerting Failures
owasp_webapp_a09_overview_desc: Without sufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting, breaches cannot be detected in a timely manner. Insufficient logging, ineffective integration with incident response systems, and lack of real-time alerting allow attackers to further attack systems, maintain persistence, and tamper with or extract data.
owasp_webapp_a09_risk_desc: Without proper logging, attackers can operate undetected for extended periods. Most breach studies show the average time to detect a breach exceeds 200 days, often discovered by external parties rather than internal monitoring.
owasp_webapp_a09_check1: Log all authentication attempts, access control failures, and input validation errors
owasp_webapp_a09_check2: Use a centralized log management system with tamper protection
owasp_webapp_a09_check3: Implement real-time alerting for suspicious activity patterns
owasp_webapp_a09_check4: Establish an incident response plan with defined escalation procedures
owasp_webapp_a10_heading: 🔟 A10 - Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
owasp_webapp_a10_overview_desc: New in 2025. Applications that improperly handle errors, exceptions, and edge cases can expose sensitive information, enter inconsistent states, or create exploitable conditions. This includes verbose error messages in production, uncaught exceptions that bypass security controls, and race conditions.
owasp_webapp_a10_risk_desc: Improper error handling can expose stack traces, database details, or internal paths. Race conditions in security checks can allow time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) attacks, bypassing authorization or payment validation.
owasp_webapp_a10_check1: Never expose stack traces, database errors, or internal paths in production
owasp_webapp_a10_check2: Implement a global error handler that returns generic messages with reference IDs
owasp_webapp_a10_check3: Test for race conditions in security-critical operations
owasp_webapp_a10_check4: Ensure all error paths maintain security invariants and clean up resources
owasp_webapp_table_a01_mitigation: Server-side access checks, deny by default, record ownership
owasp_webapp_table_a02_mitigation: Hardening process, security headers, disable defaults
owasp_webapp_table_a05_mitigation: Parameterized queries, output encoding, input validation
owasp_webapp_table_a06_mitigation: Threat modeling, secure patterns, abuse case testing
owasp_webapp_table_a07_mitigation: MFA, brute force protection, session rotation
owasp_webapp_table_a09_mitigation: Centralized logging, real-time alerts, incident response
owasp_webapp_table_a10_mitigation: Global error handler, generic messages, TOCTOU prevention
